key_insight: A lightweight transfer-learning upgrade to BiSeNetV2 makes seam segmentation far more robust to harsh reflections in robotic welding, without increasing model size or speed cost.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical real-time perception module for construction welding robots that need reliable seam detection on reflective metal surfaces.
method: Fine-tuned BiSeNetV2 with transfer learning for seam segmentation
Used a hybrid Cross-Entropy + Lovász loss to improve boundary and overlap learning
Kept the architecture lightweight to preserve FLOPs, parameters, and inference speed
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline and recovering 96.33% of severe zero-IoU failures under reflective conditions.
watch_item: Performance gains are demonstrated on the reported welding datasets and conditions, so robustness should still be validated for new site-specific lighting, joint types, and camera setups.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
